description: Authoritative Rhineland glyph contract for the Hildegard Neumes font project. Single source of truth for glyph names, advance widths, SMuFL cross-references, and documentation. Consumed by scripts/generate-rhena-glyphs.py to emit the Rust Glyph table for Rhena adoption. 2026-04-19 trace-at-scale workflow adopted for calligraphic atoms: each glyph's width is measured directly by tracing the manuscript reference image scaled to align with the font's staff-space guidelines (1 ss = 250 du). First measured widths: rh_punctum 160 (body 130), rh_virga 120 (body 91 at the head). Tradition-agnostic geometric widths remain Bravura-aligned per ADR-0009. Calligraphic atoms retain Rhineland-distinct widths per ADR-0008. Units are design units (du); 1 staff space = 250 du in our 1000-UPM scale. 2026-04-20 atom inventory expanded 19→22 atoms per ADR-0008 paleographic fidelity. rh_pes_short (integrated, for interval of a 2nd), rh_pes_foot and rh_pes_head (composition partners with rh_pes_line for interval of 3rd or wider). The split is forced by the 125-du 2nd interval being smaller than the usable sum of foot+head stub lengths in the compositional path; integrated variant matches Greciliae's continuous-gesture approach for the smallest intervals. The Hildegard pes is paleographically distinct from a generic punctum+virga stack (bottom is a hook, top lacks a virga terminator hook). Widths finalized 2026-04-20 from user traces: rh_pes_short 90, rh_pes_foot 90, rh_pes_head 90, rh_pes_line 12 (advance kept from placeholder — compositional atom, Rhena stamps at x=0 independent of advance). Stem channel for foot/line/head is uniformly x=[36, 76] (40 du thick), traced from the same manuscript reference (image 14, tall pes) for seamless compositional joins. Pes family entries include `compose_anchors` metadata declaring pitch-anchor and stem-joint points for Rhena's resolver — pitch anchors follow the pen-centerline-crosses-pitch-line convention (origin at local y=0 for bottom-pitch atoms and top-pitch atoms), joint points are the stem-stub endpoints where rh_pes_line meets rh_pes_foot/rh_pes_head. 2026-04-20 flexa family added paralleling the pes: rh_flexa_short (integrated 2nd), rh_flexa_head, rh_flexa_foot, and rh_flexa_line (redesigned from the abandoned diagonal to a vertical stem). Flexa widths finalized 90/90/90/12 from user trace-at-scale from images 16 (short) and 17 (long). Atom count 22→25. Flexa family uses its own stem-channel convention distinct from pes: flexa stems sit at x=[31, 69] (38 du thick) whereas pes stems sit at x=[36, 76] (40 du thick); trace-at-scale from different reference images yielded these per-family values. Flexa atoms use an origin-at-top convention (origin = upper attachment point) rather than origin-at-pitch like pes, mirroring the descending gesture's pen-direction: rh_flexa_head origin at top pitch, rh_flexa_line origin at top/tip, rh_flexa_foot origin at stem-top/line-joint (NOT at bottom pitch — that lives inside the foot body). Pes vs flexa minimum compositional interval differs: pes composition works for 3rd+ (stubs total 151 du < 250 du), whereas flexa composition is tight for 3rd (stubs total 244 du, leaving only 6 du for the line at 250-du interval) and progressively roomier for 4th+. 2026-04-20 post-Rhena-ADR-0012 revision: (a) compose_anchors coordinate frame is Y-up em-square, 1000 UPM, in each atom's local frame — origin (0, 0) varies per family convention but the anchors themselves give absolute landmark positions, abstracting the convention for consumers; (b) all compositional atoms now normalize pitch_x = stem_x within each family (pes=56, flexa=50) so that stamping at a pitch anchor aligns stems automatically into a single vertical column — no implicit inter-note x-offset to reverse-engineer; (c) new stem_channel {x_min, x_max} field on compositional atoms gives the x-band of the stem for Rhena's native SVG <line> rendering under ADR-0012 (thickness derivable from x_max - x_min); (d) PUA codepoints for all Rhineland non-SMuFL atoms consolidated into the SMuFL extension block U+F400..F40F for Verovio/LilyPond/@font-face interop — PRESSUS retains U+F403, pes family occupies U+F400..F402 + U+F404, flexa family occupies U+F405..F408.

coordinate_system.comment: Font authored Y-up; Rhena's SVG backend flips to Y-down at render time via a transform. y=0 in the local em frame coincides with the staff midline of the 4-line chant staff (lines at ±125, ±375; 1 staff space = 250 du; 1000 UPM). The per-atom `origin` field expresses how each atom's local (0,0) maps to screen-space cursor position — see per-enum-value definitions below. See hildegard-neumes rhena_integration_plan.md § 3.3 for the Rhena-side y-flip transform.

pitch-anchor.description: (0,0) in the atom's local em frame coincides with the atom's musical pitch landmark. Consumer positions the SVG <text> cursor at (cursor_x, screen_y_of_pitch); cursor_x advances by the atom's hmtx width, cursor_y is set by the consumer's pitch-to-screen-y mapping for the staff. Internal y-extent of geometry relative to (0,0) is given by `y_extents`. Used by all single-note atoms, pes_short/foot/head, flexa_short/head, and both clefs (where the clef's designated line IS the pitch landmark).

staff-fixed.description: (0,0) in the atom's local em frame coincides with the staff midline reference. Consumer positions the cursor at (cursor_x, screen_y_of_staff_midline) — y is constant per line of music, independent of any pitch. Geometry sits at intrinsic y ranges relative to the midline (e.g., divisio_minima at y ∈ [+250, +500] = above the staff). Used by structural / rule atoms with no pitch: divisios and virgula.

composed.description: (0,0) in the atom's local em frame coincides with a stem-channel join landmark declared in `compose_anchors` (`base`/`tip`/`stem_top`/`stem_bottom`). Consumer's composition engine positions the cursor at the join supplied by the previous atom in the compositional gesture — no pitch lookup at this stamp, no advance positioning. If the atom carries a pitch landmark (e.g., flexa_foot.pitch_bottom at local y=-119), it lives INSIDE the body and is exposed via a separate `compose_anchors` entry for downstream consumers (syllable text placement, etc.); pitch correctness is a construction invariant of the composition pass, not a stamp position. Used by pes_line, flexa_line, flexa_foot.

liquescent_direction_definitions.ascending: Maps to Rhena IR `Liquescent::Ascending` (epiphonus / pes liquescens / Dendermonde 'pinnosa').
liquescent_direction_definitions.descending: Maps to Rhena IR `Liquescent::Descending` (cephalicus / flexa liquescens).
liquescent_direction_definitions.diminutive: Maps to Rhena IR `Liquescent::Diminutive` (chantPunctumDeminutum / phrase-final softening).
liquescent_direction_definitions.null: Atom is not a liquescent terminator (default for 22 of 25 atoms).

glyphs.doc: Rhineland punctum: calligraphic parallelogram mark (body + tail); basic single note. Width 160 du (body 130) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from punctum.png, matching Bravura chantPunctum convention.

Rhineland virga: calligraphic head + thin descending stem + small rightward foot hook. Width 120 du (head 91 wide) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from virda.png; virga head is notably narrower than the punctum when traced at staff scale.

Small tilted diamond for climacus descending notes. Sharper left/right points than rh_punctum. Width 140 du (body 110 × 65, ~85% of rh_punctum width) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from climacus.png. Rhena's resolver stamps this atom N times at descending y-positions to form climacus compounds; this glyph is a single inclinatum, not a cascade.

Rhineland quilisma: compact rising ornamental gesture with rounded humps. Width 280 du (body 247 × 276) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from quilisma.png — notably larger and more vertical than the earlier 'flat 3:1 ribbon' spec, which was inherited from the abandoned module and not paleographically verified.
Ornamental repercussion note; small punctum body with a wavy tail. Directional split deferred to v2.

Rhineland apostropha: small calligraphic mark with asymmetric flick. Rhena's resolver stamps this atom 1× (apostropha), 2× (bistropha), or 3× (tristropha); the glyph itself is a single mark. Width 110 du (body 73×54, daintier than inclinatum) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from the Lomer Hildegard-Symphonia transcription chart (row 5). Advance includes extra breathing room (~37 du) for readable gaps between stamped repeats.

Rhineland pressus: vertical line attached to a short undulating line (Gardiner p. 94). No SMuFL equivalent; PUA codepoint U+F403 within the Rhineland non-SMuFL block U+F400..F40F (SMuFL extension range). Width 240 du (body 207 × 219) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from pressus.png.

Ascending liquescent terminal; small head + upward calligraphic tail. Lighter stroke per Gardiner p. 92. Semantically the Dendermonde 'pinnosa' / pes liquescens / epiphonus (maps to Rhena IR `Liquescent::Ascending` / `Epiphonus`): the upper head of an ascending two-note neume rendered as a diminutum/lighter-stroke variant. PLACEHOLDER pending trace-at-scale from Riesencodex f. 470r region (Gardiner Fig. 6 basic-neume plate); calibrate visually against Dendermonde counterpart at integration time per rhena-coordination 2026-04-26.

Rhineland cephalicus: round-loop head + descending calligraphic curl. Two contours (outer + inner void for the loop's eye). Width 150 du (body 120×238) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from a manuscript crop. Supersedes the earlier brief's 'angular head' description — Rhineland cephalicus has a rounded bulb-like head, not a tilted parallelogram.
Small diminutive punctum for diminutive-liquescent positions.

Rhineland movable C clef: compact calligraphic form (dash above a thick-thin C opening right). Width 150 du (body 122×158) set 2026-04-19 via trace-at-scale from c_clef.png — replaces the earlier Bravura placeholder. Significantly more compact than Bravura chantCclef (134×442) because the Rhineland C clef is a smaller scribal mark than a square-notation engraved one. LSB-0 origin; the C body sits above and below y=0 (the line the clef designates).
Rhineland F clef for low-range antiphons.
Short phrase break; thin bar spanning one staff space.
Medium phrase break; thin bar spanning the full 4-line staff.
Long phrase break; thin bar extending above and below the staff.
Final double bar; two equal-weight 16-du verticals separated by an 88-du gap (total 120 du). SMuFL-aligned 2026-04-19.
Breath mark above the top staff line. SMuFL-aligned to Bravura convention (width 91 du, y ∈ [+255, +500]) 2026-04-19.

Rhineland pes (integrated variant, interval of a 2nd): hook + short stem + head drawn as one continuous calligraphic gesture per Hildegard paleographic practice. Rhena's resolver stamps this for pes-of-2nd; wider intervals compose foot+line+head instead. Split from the compositional path 2026-04-20 because combined foot/head stubs exceed the 125-du 2nd interval. Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 13). Pitch anchors normalized to stem column x=56 for compositional consistency with the pes family.

Rhineland pes foot: bottom hook + small upward stem stub. Composition partner for rh_pes_line + rh_pes_head. Used only for pes-of-3rd-or-wider (2nds are handled by rh_pes_short). Paleographic fidelity per ADR-0008 — the Hildegard pes bottom is a hook, not a Rhineland punctum parallelogram. Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 14). Pitch anchor normalized to stem column x=56 so composed stems render as a single vertical line.

Rhineland pes head: calligraphic head + rightward foot flick + small downward stem stub. Explicitly NOT rh_virga: no bottom foot hook (that reads as a standalone-gesture terminator and would appear midway through a composed pes). Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 14). Pitch anchor normalized to stem column x=56 so composed stems render as a single vertical line.

Ascending stroke connector for pes/scandicus joins. Private, PUA U+F404 in the Rhineland non-SMuFL block. In pes composition, stretched vertically by Rhena to fill the interval between rh_pes_foot's stem_top and rh_pes_head's stem_bottom — or substituted by a native SVG <line> at the stem_channel x-band per Rhena's ADR-0012 resolver. Retraced 2026-04-20 at x=[36, 76] (40 du thick). Advance 12 is a placeholder value kept unchanged because the atom is compositional-only (Rhena stamps at x=0, not along a text run).

Rhineland flexa (integrated variant, interval of a 2nd descending): one continuous 'n'-shape gesture covering both notes. Rhena's resolver stamps this for flexa-of-2nd; wider intervals compose rh_flexa_head + rh_flexa_line + rh_flexa_foot. Mirrors rh_pes_short architecture. Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 16). Pitch anchors normalized to stem column x=50 for compositional consistency with the flexa family.

Rhineland flexa head: calligraphic head at top of descending gesture + downward stem stub. Composition partner for rh_flexa_line + rh_flexa_foot. Used only for flexa-of-3rd-or-wider (2nds use rh_flexa_short). Distinct from rh_pes_head because Hildegard's flexa head is a pen-entry gesture whereas pes head is a pen-exit flick. Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 17). Stem stub is 125 du (reaches from y=-42 head-junction to y=-125 line-joint) — longer than rh_pes_head's 80-du stub because flexa's stem proportionally carries the descending gesture's weight. Pitch anchor normalized to stem column x=50 so composed flexa stems render as a single vertical line (rather than the diagonal that would result from implicit pitch/stem x-offset).

Rhineland flexa foot: downward stem stub + foot hook terminator at the bottom of a descending gesture. Composition partner for rh_flexa_head + rh_flexa_line. Distinct from rh_pes_foot because Hildegard's flexa foot is a pen-exit terminator whereas pes foot is a pen-entry hook (different taper dynamics). Width 90 traced at scale from manuscript reference (image 17). Glyph origin sits at the TOP of the outline (line-joint attachment point); stem stub descends from y=0 to y=-75, foot hook body extends further to y=-163. Bottom-pitch anchor lives inside the foot hook body at (50, -119) — stem column x matching stem_top anchor, y at pen-centerline crossing.

Vertical stretchable connector for flexa compositions. Private, PUA U+F408 in the Rhineland non-SMuFL block. Geometry similar to rh_pes_line but oriented downward: 38-du stem at x=[31, 69], 125 du tall extending from y=0 (top, tip) to y=-125 (bottom, base). Stretched vertically to fill the gap between rh_flexa_head's stem_bottom and rh_flexa_foot's stem_top — or substituted by a native SVG <line> at the stem_channel x-band per Rhena's ADR-0012 resolver. 2026-04-20: redesigned from the abandoned-rhineland.rs diagonal parallelogram (172×260) to a vertical stem after paleographic review of image 17 confirmed Hildegard's flexa stem is vertical. Advance 12 is a placeholder — compositional-only, Rhena stamps at x=0 independent of advance.
